d83fd9920e604e3182f7c5ea4fcee4c2.reason:
A brutal civil war has been raging in Sudan since April 2023 between two rival military factions: the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF), led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF), commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan "Hemedti" Dagalo. The conflict has resulted in the world's largest humanitarian crisis, with widespread displacement, famine conditions, and accusations of ethnic cleansing and war crimes. 
Causes of the Conflict
The current war stems from a power struggle that followed the 2019 ousting of long-time dictator Omar al-Bashir. A transitional government was established with a plan to move towards civilian, democratic rule, led jointly by al-Burhan (head of the army) and Hemedti (head of the powerful RSF paramilitary). Tensions escalated over plans to integrate the 100,000-strong RSF into the regular army and determine who would lead the unified force, with both generals vying for ultimate power and control over lucrative economic resources like gold mines. Fighting erupted on April 15, 2023, when the RSF was redeployed around the country in a move the army viewed as a threat.
